Assembly: The assembly ilMelNeva1.hap1.1 is based on 24x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome W1 (3,508 - 19,093 Kbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome W2 (485 - 9,878 Kbp) are unknown.The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 28 (1,156 - 2,655 Kbp) are unknown.Chromosome Z was identified through read coverage and synteny analysis with Melitaea diamina (GCA_964264535.1).

Assembly: The assembly iyLasLatv2.1 is based on 34x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly iyLasMori1.2 is based on 35x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA2. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Several repeat types can be seen to cluster by Hi-C yet have no association with the defined chromosomes. These repeats make up a large proportion of the assembly.

Assembly: The assembly ioArcGran1.1 is based on 37x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The X chromosome was identified by its half PacBio read coverage relative to the autosomes and confirmed via synteny analysis with?Hetaerina titia?(GCA_037158775.1).cThe exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 1 (19,695 - 25,152 & 109,206 - 127,429 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 2 (5,615 - 6,187 & 92,561 - 93,593 Kbp) are unknown.
